text: Handling deadlines is a crucial skill for both work and study. Effective time management helps you break large tasks into smaller steps, so you can work steadily without last-minute stress. Prioritization is also important: focus first on the most urgent or important tasks, and leave less critical work for later. When a project feels overwhelming, scope management can help. This means defining what is included in your work and avoiding extra tasks that take too much time. Finally, communication is key. If you see a deadline might be missed, talk to your manager or teacher early. They may offer help, adjust the timeline, or simplify the requirements.
